chat.author: Nancy Cam-Winget
chat.text: Hopefully those remote can hear us ok!

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: technically OAuth did SD-JWT before SPICE started working on SD-CWT, so depending on token format or tie in to OAUTH, you might wish to discuss with them as well.

chat.author: Muhammad Usama Sardar
chat.text: I do not believe latency is genuine critique, because you have to do it continuously.

chat.author: Markus Rudy
chat.text: Leaving my comment here: in practice, we see evidence production taking time on the order of magnitude of inter-continental network roundtrips. But what's even worse is verification, which usually involves remote calls to the hardware vendor authorities and dwarves all other latency concerns.

chat.author: Nathanael Ritz
chat.text: Thanks Markus. Another reason why I think we should seriously look at exploring solutions for per-connection binding to cached Attestation Results, as these speed issues are just as challenging for Post-Handshake in the Background-Check model as it would be for Intra-Handshake. In either case, I think we need to seriously consider performance impacts that RA imposes for sure.

chat.author: Muhammad Usama Sardar
chat.text:
Markus Rudy said:

Leaving my comment here: in practice, we see evidence production taking time on the order of magnitude of inter-continental network roundtrips. But what's even worse is verification, which usually involves remote calls to the hardware vendor authorities and dwarves all other latency concerns.

As I said, one has to account that saving just a first round trip is not very helpful when you have to do continuously.

chat.author: Ionuț Mihalcea
chat.text: It's not necessary for it to be a secret because your evidence should prove that the Attester only issues evidence through a well defined path, and an attacker can't coerce the Attester into generating evidence with random bitstrings.

chat.author: Muhammad Usama Sardar
chat.text: Other than this, there are 3 solutions already :slight_smile:

chat.author: Nathanael Ritz
chat.text: Ah, Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy, my beloved.

chat.author: Thomas Fossati
chat.text: @tiru @hannes, I like this. Thanks for addressing https://github.com/tls-attestation/exported-attestation/issues/56 with a fully-fledged proposal

chat.author: Muhammad Usama Sardar
chat.text: Thanks everyone for the feedback and insights.

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: You can always ask for an AI review in the style of an HTTP Dir reviewer
